finding_category: Pulmonary nodules — perifissural lymph node characterization
anatomical_location: Right middle lobe, right lower lobe, left lower lobe
doctor_description: RML solid nodule 4mm (image 137), RLL solid nodule 4mm (image 159), LLL solid subpleural nodule 5mm (image 210). Impression: per Fleischner, high-risk patients CT 12 months; low-risk no follow-up.
validator_description: RML 4mm — perifissural lymph node, no follow-up. RLL 4mm — likely intrapulmonary lymph node, no follow-up. LLL 5mm — triangular, likely intrapulmonary lymph node, no follow-up.
management_impact: Validator identifies all three nodules as likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes requiring no follow-up. Doctor applies standard Fleischner with possible 12-month CT for high-risk patients. No definitive management change but validator's characterization eliminates follow-up uncertainty. Measure 364.
notes: All three nodules are perifissural/intrapulmonary lymph nodes per validator — these specifically require no follow-up per Fleischner 2017. Doctor's generic Fleischner statement is less precise.

anatomical_location: Segmental and subsegmental bronchi, lower lobes
validator_description: Wall thickening and secretions of the segmental and subsegmental bronchi of lower lobes, to a lesser extent in upper lobes. Small amount of mucus in trachea and main bronchi. Tiny tracheal diverticula. May represent bronchitis.
management_impact: No management change — clinically reportable airway finding omitted by doctor. Relevant in context of dyspnea/asthma indication.
notes: Doctor completely omits bronchial wall thickening and secretions. Relevant given asthma/dyspnea indication.

doctor_description: Single liver lesion -1 HU, 1.0×0.7cm. ACR White Paper: high-risk MRI 3-6m, low-risk no follow-up.
validator_description: Two liver lesions -1 HU up to 8mm. ACR White Paper: high-risk MRI 3-6m, low-risk no follow-up.
management_impact: Both apply same risk-stratified management. Doctor counts 1 lesion, validator counts 2; size difference minor. Management conclusion identical.
notes: Minor count and size discrepancy. Follow-up algorithm identical.

anatomical_location: Humeral heads and T9 vertebral body
validator_description: Small bone islands of the humeral heads and T9 vertebral body (images 177, 179).
management_impact: No management change — benign incidental findings requiring no follow-up.
notes: Incidental bone islands omitted by doctor. No clinical consequence.

doctor_description: Regression of some previously identified nodules.
validator_description: Complete regression of previously identified nodules in the lower lobes of both lungs.
management_impact: No management difference. Both agree no follow-up needed per Fleischner. Wording difference only.
notes: Partial vs complete regression — same management conclusion.

doctor_description: Left kidney lesion 1.1×0.7×0.9cm, density 44 HU, with negative interval change vs March 17 2025. CT or MRI without/with contrast recommended.
validator_description: Left kidney lesion 1.1×0.7×0.9cm, density 44 HU. Previously 0.6×0.6cm — interval growth. CT or MRI without/with contrast recommended.
management_impact: Doctor states 'negative interval change' (i.e., worsening/enlargement) — ambiguous language that could be read as either worsening or stability. Validator explicitly documents prior size (0.6×0.6cm) and confirms growth. Both recommend contrast imaging but validator's documentation of growth is clinically essential for urgency framing.
notes: Doctor's 'negative interval change' is ambiguous; validator provides prior size and documents growth definitively. Both recommend CT/MRI but growth documentation changes the clinical weight of the recommendation.

doctor_description: Mild pulmonary emphysema. LDCT lung cancer screening recommended if eligible; current study may be used as baseline.
management_impact: Doctor adds LDCT recommendation not found in validator. Validator mentions emphysema in findings but makes no screening recommendation. Both are acceptable; doctor's addition is appropriate for eligible patients.
notes: Doctor includes QMM23-appropriate LDCT language; validator omits. Doctor's addition is correct clinical practice. Graded as overreport only in the sense that validator does not include it, but clinically appropriate.

validator_description: Coronary artery calcification identified in the right coronary artery (image 135), qualitatively assessed as mild. PCP consultation recommended.
management_impact: Doctor explicitly states no coronary calcification — directly contradicted by validator who identifies mild CAC in RCA. ACRad44 completely failed: no CAC documentation and no PCP referral. Management change: PCP referral required.
notes: ACRad44 violation — doctor actively reports absence of CAC when validator finds mild CAC in RCA. False negative documented finding.

validator_description: No cardiomegaly, mild enlargement of the left ventricle.
management_impact: No immediate management change from CT alone. Incidental LV enlargement omitted.
notes: Mild LV enlargement noted only by validator. Clinically relevant but not independently actionable.

validator_description: Minimal bronchial wall thickening of subsegmental bronchi — may represent bronchitis.
management_impact: No management change. Clinically reportable airway finding omitted by doctor.
notes: Minimal bronchial thickening omitted. Relevant in chronic cough/chest pain indication context.

anatomical_location: Left costal pleura, level of posterior segment 10th rib
doctor_description: Localized thickening of the left costal pleura, up to 5mm, length ~3.4cm. Impression: may be postinflammatory.
management_impact: No management change — pleural thickening attributed to postinflammatory process. Validator does not confirm. Overcall with no consequence.
notes: Doctor documents pleural thickening not confirmed by validator. Postinflammatory attribution — no management impact.

doctor_description: Minimal left pleural effusion, including along the interlobar pleura.
management_impact: Study performed with incomplete inspiration and respiratory artifacts — validator attributes pleural findings to technique. Overcall in suboptimal study. No management change.
notes: Study performed without full inspiration. Validator explicitly states no pleural effusion. Doctor's minimal effusion overcall likely reflects respiratory artifact.

management_impact: 7mm pericardial effusion is a clinically relevant finding missed by doctor. In context of lung cancer (indication: malignant neoplasm of right lung), pericardial effusion has additional significance. No immediate CT-triggered management change but clinically important documentation.
notes: Doctor misses 7mm pericardial effusion. Relevant in lung cancer context. No acute imaging workup change from CT alone.

doctor_description: Moderate coronary artery calcification. No explicit PCP recommendation in impression.
validator_description: Moderate CAC in LAD and LCx. PCP consultation recommended.
management_impact: ACRad44 PCP referral language absent from doctor's impression. Validator includes it.
notes: ACRad44 MIPS violation — PCP recommendation absent.

doctor_description: Single enlarged lower paratracheal lymph node up to 1.2cm short axis (series 4, image 72), no change.
management_impact: Validator explicitly states no adenopathy. Study has marked respiratory artifacts — enlarged node may be artifact or not confirmed. Doctor notes no change from prior. In lung cancer patient, overcalling adenopathy can affect staging. No immediate management change from this finding alone.
notes: Validator says no adenopathy; doctor reports 1.2cm lower paratracheal node. Suboptimal study may contribute. In lung cancer patient, this is a relevant overcall.

doctor_description: Aortic and mitral valve calcifications mentioned — wait, doctor does mention 'Multiple calcifications of the aortic arch and aortic valve' in great vessels.
management_impact: No management change — incidental valve calcification omission.
notes: Doctor documents aortic valve calcification but omits mitral. Validator documents both.

doctor_description: Subsolid nodule up to 9mm (image 139) — not previously present. Per Fleischner: consider CT 3 months, PET/CT, or biopsy.
validator_description: Ground glass nodules up to 7mm (images 139, 162) — not previously present. New solid nodules in left lung. For low-risk: CT 3-6 months; for high-risk: CT 3-6 months then 18-24 months.
management_impact: Doctor describes single 9mm subsolid nodule; validator identifies GGNs up to 7mm plus additional solid nodule. Size and character discrepancy. Doctor's Fleischner recommendation (PET/CT or biopsy) is more aggressive than validator's CT 3-6m. No definitive management change but validator's approach is more measured.
notes: Measurement and nodule count difference in LLL. Doctor's more aggressive recommendation for a single nodule vs validator's tiered approach for multiple. Measure 364.

management_impact: New 6mm solid LUL nodule missed by doctor. Per Fleischner 2017 for new solid 6mm: CT 3-6m or 18-24m depending on risk. Documentation gap.
notes: New LUL solid 6mm nodule completely missed. Should be included in Fleischner management.

doctor_description: Mild coronary calcification. PCP recommendation stated.
validator_description: Coronary artery calcifications in LM, LAD, and LCx — qualitatively assessed as moderate. PCP recommendation stated.
management_impact: Both include PCP recommendation — ACRad44 met by both. Severity discrepancy (mild vs moderate, 3-vessel) may affect PCP's risk assessment.
notes: ACRad44 satisfied by both. Severity underestimation by doctor (mild vs moderate, 3-vessel disease).

validator_description: Wall thickening of subsegmental bronchi with secretion — may represent inflammatory bronchitis.
management_impact: No management change — clinically reportable airway finding omitted.
notes: Bronchial wall thickening with secretion omitted by doctor.
